game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **异星要塞 - 弹幕街机塔防+Rougelike随机地牢**: "异星要塞" is a top-down bullet-hell roguelike where players control a customizable mech vehicle, navigating randomly generated dungeons by dragging a finger across the screen to move, while equipped gun turrets automatically fire at incoming alien enemies. The core loop involves surviving waves of enemies, dodging dense bullet patterns, and collecting resources dropped by defeated foes to temporarily enhance the mech's capabilities for the current run. Players unlock new mech bodies (10+ types), gun turrets (20+ types), and follower pets (8 types) by completing specific campaign missions or achieving high scores in endless modes, which can then be combined to form unique loadouts. Difficulty scales across 45 campaign levels and endless modes by increasing enemy spawn rates (e.g., 5-10 enemies per wave initially, scaling to 20+), bullet speeds, and introducing new enemy types with unique attack patterns. A run ends when the mech's health, typically starting at 100 HP, is depleted by enemy attacks or environmental hazards, with progression tied to permanent upgrades purchased with collected "alien tech" between runs.
game_info.description: Navigate through procedurally generated alien dungeons in your combat mech, surviving intense bullet-hell waves. Your turrets automatically fire at enemies while you focus on dodging deadly projectiles. Collect resources from defeated aliens to upgrade your mech temporarily during your run. Reach the dungeon exit to progress through increasingly challenging levels. If your mech's health reaches zero, the run ends.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move your mech in eight directions
Space: Dash to quickly evade danger (cooldown applies)
Shift: Activate shield for temporary invulnerability (limited charges)
Z: Toggle auto-fire mode on/off
ESC: Pause/unpause the game
R: Restart (return to start screen)
ENTER: Start the game

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement and collision detection. Validates that the mech can move in all directions without going out of bounds and that position updates correctly in the game state.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the mech in a circular pattern around the center of the screen, testing all eight directions. Verify that coordinates update properly and the mech stays within canvas boundaries.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The mech should smoothly move in all directions, stay within the play area, and gameState.player position should accurately reflect screen position. Test passes if no errors occur and movement is fluid.

TEST_1.expected: The mech should smoothly move in all directions, stay within the play area, and gameState.player position should accurately reflect screen position. Test passes if no errors occur and movement is fluid.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests winning condition by completing a level. Validates combat mechanics, enemy defeat, resource collection, and level progression to trigger GAME_OVER_WIN.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Actively engage enemies using optimal positioning to maximize turret effectiveness. Dodge bullet patterns efficiently while collecting dropped resources. Use dash and shield strategically to survive and reach the exit portal.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully defeat all enemies in waves, collect resources, reach the exit portal, and progress through at least 3 levels to trigger win condition. Test succeeds when gameState.gamePhase becomes "GAME_OVER_WIN".

TEST_3.test_description: Tests defensive abilities and survival mechanics. Validates dash cooldown system, shield activation, and temporary invulnerability frames.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Intentionally navigate into dense enemy fire while cycling through defensive abilities. Use dash to evade, then activate shield when available, monitoring cooldown timers and invulnerability states.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Dash and shield should provide temporary invulnerability as intended. Cooldowns should prevent ability spam. Player should survive longer with proper ability usage compared to no defensive actions.

TEST_4.test_description: Tests resource collection and temporary upgrade system. Validates that defeating enemies drops collectibles and that picking them up enhances mech capabilities.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Focus on defeating enemies and collecting all dropped resources. Monitor stat changes in gameState to verify that upgrades are applied correctly and stack appropriately.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Resources should spawn after enemy deaths, be collectable by the player, and provide visible/measurable improvements to mech stats like fire rate or damage. Upgrades should persist for the current run only.
